---
title: "Day 1: Understanding the Data Before Choosing the Story"
description: "The beginning of my research process for the Pacific Dataviz Challenge 2026."
author: "Matteo Arellano"
date: "2026-07-19"
categories:
  - Research Log
  - Dataset Exploration
  - Pacific Dataviz Challenge
toc: true
---

## Today's question

> What data is available, and what kinds of questions could it genuinely help me answer?

## What I am working on

Today, I am beginning my first exploration of the datasets available for the Pacific Dataviz Challenge 2026.

Before choosing a story or creating a polished visualization, I want to understand the evidence properly.

My first task is to identify:

- What each dataset measures
- What each row represents
- Which countries, territories or communities are included
- Which years are covered
- Which variables could be useful
- How much information is missing
- Which datasets could potentially be connected

## Why I am starting here

It would be easy to begin with an attractive map or chart. However, a visualization can look convincing while representing the data incorrectly.

I first want to understand what the datasets can support and what they cannot support.

## Early observations

I will add my first observations here after inspecting the datasets.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Exploratory work

The ideas, calculations and visualizations documented during this phase are provisional. They may change as I improve the data cleaning and methodology.
:::

## Questions that emerged

I will record new questions here as I explore the data.

## Next step

Create a dataset inventory covering the source, unit of analysis, geographic coverage, time period, important variables and possible uses of each dataset.
